level_signals: at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
Ability to earn the respect of Staff and Principal-level engineers and researchers
Comp range $232K-$282K base, consistent with Staff/Principal PM level at Airbnb

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
Track record of owning strategy and roadmap for technically complex systems — not just managing features, but setting direction and making hard prioritization calls
Strong experimentation fluency: comfortable designing A/B tests, interpreting counterfactual results, and reasoning about proxy metrics vs. long-term outcomes
Experience working on two-sided marketplaces or platforms where you balanced supply-side and demand-side tradeoffs
Ability to earn the respect of Staff and Principal-level engineers and researchers — you don't need to write code, but you need to engage at a level that builds credibility
Clear, structured communicator who can distill complex technical tradeoffs for executives and translate strategic intent into actionable guidance for engineers

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with LLM-based products in production — ideally involving evaluation challenges, latency constraints, or safety and guardrail work (preferred)
Familiarity with reinforcement learning, contextual bandits, or explore/exploit frameworks in a product context (preferred)
Background working alongside applied researchers or with teams that publish externally (preferred)

explicitly_excluded: Candidates located in: Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, Rhode Island

scope.surface: Relevance & Personalization platform — search ranking, recommendations, personalization, and generative AI across the guest journey and host marketplace
scope.business_model: two-sided marketplace (B2C / B2B), travel and short-term rental
primary_metrics_inferred: Bookings driven by relevance and personalization
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the roadmap for Airbnb's relevance and personalization platform
Make prioritization calls balancing guest experience, host success, revenue, fairness, and marketplace health
Partner with ML engineers and applied researchers to shape model strategy, evaluation frameworks, and experimentation design
Align cross-functional partners on shared goals and sequencing
Drive the Tripcycle vision connecting R&P intelligence across the full trip lifecycle
Design and oversee A/B experiments with rigorous metric design and long-term effect measurement
Translate complex technical tradeoffs into clear decisions and narratives for leadership
Stay close to guests and hosts via user research, marketplace data, and competitive signals

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potential additional component alongside bonus and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($232K-$282K base) with equity and bonus upside
Clear product surface with meaningful scope — Airbnb's matching intelligence engine at global scale
Explicit articulation of the team's mission and north star (travel companion, not just search engine)
Preferred qualifications clearly labeled, reducing ambiguity about what is hard vs. soft
Encourages broadest talent pool; disability accommodation process stated
red_flags: Role requires physical presence in San Francisco, CA — fully remote candidates ineligible
Significant state exclusion list (11 states) limits eligible US-based applicants
10+ years PM experience with 3+ years in ML/AI at scale is a high bar that narrows the candidate pool considerably
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirements; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and company context)
ambiguities: JD states 'need to be based in San Francisco, CA' suggesting onsite, but does not explicitly state percentage of in-office time required — treated as onsite.
The exclusion state list is described as 'continuously being updated' — actual eligibility may differ at time of application.
Seniority level label ('Staff / Principal PM') is inferred from compensation and required years; Airbnb does not use those exact titles in this JD.
